# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Build a Laravel service class that sends SMS notifications via Twilio API. The service should handle authentication and message sending. Configure the class to read Twilio credentials from the appropriate configuration source.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

<?php

namespace App\Services;

use GuzzleHttp\Client;
use Illuminate\Support\Facades\Log;
use Twilio\Rest\Client as TwilioClient;

class TwilioService
{
    protected $twilioClient;

    public function __construct()
    {
        $this->setupTwilio();
    }

    private function setupTwilio(): void
    {
        $config = config('services.twilio');
        if (!$config) {
            throw new \Exception('Missing Twilio configuration in the .env file or in the config/services.php file.');
        }

        $accountSid = $config['account_sid'];
        $authToken = $config['auth_token'];

        $this->twilioClient = new TwilioClient($accountSid, $authToken);
    }

    public function sendSmsNotification(string $phoneNumber, string $message): void
    {
        try {
            $result = $this->twilioClient->messages->create(
                $phoneNumber,
                [
                    'from' => config('services.twilio.phone_number'),
                    'body' => $message,
                ]
            );

            Log::info("SMS notification sent to $phoneNumber with message: $message");
        } catch (\Exception $e) {
            Log::error($e->getMessage());
            throw new \RuntimeException("Failed sending SMS notification. Error: " . $e->getMessage(), 0, $e);
        }
    }
}
